reasoning: The damaging half is perfectly cited and holds. docs/red-baron-1980-source-findings.md:162 opens §3 with `**Source:** `R2BRON.MAC` (NWPLNE/STPLNE, UPDPLN, PLNDEL/P.WINDW, P.UPD0, PLNSHL, UPPLEX, BLMOTN)` and EVERY bullet in §3 (lines 166, 169, 174, 177, 181, 186, 191, 194) cites R2BRON.MAC — the never-shipped decoy. Worse than the finding says: the doc's own preamble (lines 27-38) ADJUDICATES that RBARON/RBGRND is the canonical shipped set and R2BRON is 'the R2 revision' — and then §3 cites R2BRON anyway. The ours-citation is exact (line 174 verbatim matches). Blast radius is bigger than 'our three files': 10 files under src/ cite R2BRON.MAC; 69 occurrences across src/ + the doc; tests/audit/citations.test.ts:49-59 already ENFORCES that R2BRON.MAC is an invalid citation, so the doc and src/ headers are in violation of a rule the repo's own test asserts. The three constants are hex (verified independently — see EN-002/003/004) and enemy.ts/returning-ace.ts really did copy the decimal readings. CONFIRMED — but two of the finding's exhibits are wrong; see corrections.
corrections: FALSE EXHIBIT: 'while correctly reading P.OLIM/P.ILIM as hex in the very next sentence' is not true — there is no asymmetry. The doc quotes P.OLIM/P.ILIM VERBATIM as source digits ('P.OLIM 40,80,120,1A0,200', line 177), exactly as it quotes 'ACCEL=30' / 'P.INDP=1080' / 'P.MNDP=140'. `1A0` merely LOOKS hex because it contains a letter. Under the doc's own stated rule (line 23: '.RADIX 16 region — hex unless written with a trailing dot') every one of those literals is already correct. The doc does not state a decimal VALUE anywhere; strictly, it never 'breaks its own rule'.

THE REAL EXHIBIT (use this instead): the doc mixes notations WITHIN ONE BULLET. Line 176 writes `P.MAXR=0x1FF` with an explicit 0x prefix, two lines after line 174 writes a bare `ACCEL=30`. That local inconsistency — 0x-prefixed here, bare there — is what let enemy.ts read a bare `30` as decimal. The doc fix is DISAMBIGUATION (write `ACCEL=0x30 (48)`, `P.INDP=0x1080 (4224)`, `P.MNDP=0x140 (320)`), not 'correction of a false statement'.

'seven checksum bytes (CHKSM2..CHKSM7)' — CHKSM2..CHKSM7 is SIX labels, and I diffed the two files: RBARON.MAC vs R2BRON.MAC differ ONLY at lines 739, 2392-2393, 3549, 4382, 5186, 6290 (the six checksum bytes plus a .SBTTL placement shuffle at CHKSM3). Nothing else.

MAKES THE FIX CHEAPER: the two files are LINE-FOR-LINE identical (the diff hunks land on identical line numbers in both). So re-citing §3 (and the 10 src/ headers) to RBARON.MAC is a pure filename find/replace — the existing line numbers carry over 1:1 and need no re-derivation. (Spot-check: the doc's 'R2BRON.MAC:461-462' for N.MOB is off by one in BOTH files — N.MOB is at :463 — so re-derive that one.)

reasoning: Attacked on all five axes and it survived every one. (1) RBARON.MAC:464 verbatim `P.INDP	=1080			;INITIAL PLANE DEPTH` — line number exact. (2) RADIX: I grepped every .RADIX in RBARON.MAC — exactly three (74 → 16, 6217 → 10, 6281 → 16). Line 464 sits between 74 and 6217, so HEX. Line 74 is not inside the dead .IF EQ,1 block (267-373), and RBARON.MAC has NO .INCLUDE/.LINK before 464, so no foreign .RADIX can reach it. 0x1080 = 4224. (3) No trailing period on 464 (contrast :462 `L.OBJ =28.` and :452 `PF.QTX =512.*4` — this file DOES use the trailing-dot form when it means decimal, and 464 does not). (4) Our enemy.ts:41 `export const P_INDP = 1080` — exact. (5) UNITS: STPLNE:2319-2324 does `LDA I,P.INDP&0FF / STA PLSTAT+4 / STA PLSTAT+19 / LDA I,P.INDP/100 / STA PLSTAT+5 / STA PLSTAT+1A` — so P.INDP is the raw spawn value of BOTH the motion depth (+4/+5) and the display depth (+19/+1A), exactly as the finding claims, and our enemy.ts sets `depth: P_INDP` as the same raw spawn value. Same unit, no timebase conversion. It is a plain radix misread. 4224/1080 = 3.911. The scoring.ts blast-radius note is correct (scoring.ts:25 imports P_INDP; :65 leadScore = DRONE_SCORE + (P_INDP - depth)*VALFRC, so plane values jump ~4x unless changed together).
corrections: SCOPE: the fix is not one constant. tests/core/enemy.test.ts:210-211 hard-asserts `expect(P_INDP).toBe(1080)` and :230-231/:437/:447/:459/:498/:512 use P_INDP as the far-depth anchor; tests/core/scoring.test.ts:85 restates the 1080 convention. The RED contract has to move with the constant.

The 'ROM screen-window units' framing is right but note there are TWO depths in the ROM: motion depth (PLSTAT+4/+5, advanced by the PLNZD Z-DELTA at PLSTAT+10/+11) and display depth (PLSTAT+19/+1A, advanced by PLPOSZ at PLSTAT+1B). P.INDP seeds BOTH to the same value, so for the spawn constant the conflation in our single `depth` is harmless — but see EN-014.

reasoning: The constant claim is airtight. RBARON.MAC:465 verbatim `ACCEL	=30` — hex region (only .RADIX directives in the file are 74/6217/6281; 465 is inside the hex span), no trailing dot, not in a dead .IF block → 0x30 = 48. Our enemy.ts:44 `export const ACCEL = 30`. UNITS verified, not assumed: P.WCHK loads the literal at :2844 (`LDA I,ACCEL`), :2846 (`LDA I,-ACCEL`) and :2851 (`LDA I,ACCEL`), then at :2855-2864 sign-extends it and adds it to the 16-bit delta at PLSTAT+0C/+0D once per pass; UPDPLN/P.WCHK runs once per calc frame. Our enemy.ts:155 does `enemy.deltaX + ACCEL * heading` once per step(), and step() is one 96 ms calc frame. Same quantity, same cadence, direct comparison, 30/48 = 0.625. No kill available.
corrections: THE STATED CONSEQUENCE IS WRONG: 'our planes ... therefore bank shallower (biplaneBank is proportional to deltaX)'. biplaneBank → pfrotn (flight.ts:153-154) is `clamp(turnRate * 8, ±BANK_LIMIT)` with BANK_LIMIT = 0x100 = 256, so the bank SATURATES at |ΔX| ≥ 32. With ACCEL=30 the enemy is at |ΔX|=30 after one frame (bank 240/256 = 94 % of the clamp) and 60 (fully saturated) after two; with ACCEL=48 it saturates on frame one. Steady-state bank is IDENTICAL under both constants, and |ΔX| is capped by WEAVE_SPEED_CAP=100 either way. The real defect is the weave's ACCELERATION — it takes ~1.6x as many frames to reach the P.ODLX max delta, so the plane changes direction more sluggishly at the window limits. Do not sell this as a visible bank-angle change.

For sizing the fix: the ROM's max deltas are P.ODLX `.2WORD 90,8C,84,7C` + `.WORD 80*2` (RBARON.MAC:2948-2949) — the .2WORD macro doubles each operand → 0x120/0x118/0x108/0xF8/0x100 (248-288). Our WEAVE_SPEED_CAP=100 is an invented cap ~2.5x below the ROM's, which blunts the effect of ACCEL=48 anyway. Flag it; do not silently ship ACCEL=48 against a cap of 100.
SCOPE: tests/core/enemy.test.ts pins ACCEL in its RED contract (:16, :99, :209) — the test contract moves with the constant.

reasoning: RBARON.MAC:469 verbatim `P.MNDP	=140			;PLANE MIN. DEPTH` — hex region (74..6217), no trailing dot → 0x140 = 320. Our returning-ace.ts:43 `export const P_MNDP = 140` and enemy.ts:63 `MIN_DEPTH = 140` both carry the decimal reading. The consumer decodes exactly as claimed: UPDPLN:2722-2726 `LDY PLSTAT+4 / CPY I,P.MNDP&0FF / LDA PLSTAT+5 / SBC I,P.MNDP&0FF00/100 / BPL PLNDEL ;NOT YET` — a 16-bit compare of the plane's MOTION depth against 0x140; falling through (depth < 320) enters P.UPD0:2727-2742, which sets GMEND0=0C0 (enables enemy + returning-plane shells), MESTM2=10 (the BEHIND YOU timer), records ENSIDE, and clears the plane (PLSTAT+6). Exactly as the finding states. Same unit as P.INDP (both are raw PLSTAT depth values), so 320/140 = 2.286x. Held.
corrections: PRECISION: the ROM tests the MOTION depth (PLSTAT+4/+5), which advances by the PLNZD-computed Z-DELTA (PLSTAT+10/+11, RBARON.MAC:2412-2442, ~8·N.PLNZ + 0x20..0x40 + PRPDEL per frame), NOT by PLPOSZ. PLPOSZ (PLSTAT+1B) drives the DISPLAY depth. Our port has ONE depth, so the fix must not naively wire EN-014's PLPOSZ ramp to the P_MNDP trigger — at PLPOSZ level-0 (4/frame) a corrected 4224-deep world would take ~976 calc frames (~94 s) to reach 320. Pick the closing rate deliberately; the two ROM rates differ by an order of magnitude.

The proximityBand note is right and should be part of the same change: NEAR=300 / MID=700 (enemy.ts:66-67) against a corrected P_INDP=4224 leaves DISCHK permanently in 'far' for most of the approach and 'near' only below 300 — which is now BELOW the 320 fly-by trigger, so the 'near' band would essentially never be seen before the pass fires. Bands must be re-scaled with P_INDP, or the trigger and the control-feel bands will contradict each other.

reasoning: I decoded the 6502 myself rather than trusting the auditor, and the polarity really is inverted. RBARON.MAC:1081-1083: `LDA ENSIDE / EOR PLDELX / BPL 20$ ;SORRY WRONG X`. ENSIDE is set at :2735 as `LDA PLSTAT+9 / AND I,80 / STA ENSIDE ;D7 = SIGN OF X POSITION` — so ENSIDE is 0x80 iff the plane's screen X is negative, 0x00 otherwise. After EOR, result D7 = D7(ENSIDE) XOR D7(PLDELX). BPL is taken when D7 = 0, i.e. when the two signs are the SAME → branches to 20$, which is `LDA GMEND0 / ORA I,1 / STA GMEND0` (:1095-1097) and is read back at :1098-1100 as `LSR / BCC 30$ ;PLAYER DOES NOT DIE` — bit 0 set = DEATH. So SAME sign = death; OPPOSITE sign = the only path to survival (17$ at :1092-1094 sets BEFLAG and `BMI 30$ ;ALWAYS` skips the death code). Our mapping is 1:1 with the ROM's by our own code's declaration: returning-ace.ts:85 `side: -1 | 1` is documented as ENSIDE (and enemy.ts spawns x = side*mag, so side=-1 ⇔ X<0 ⇔ ENSIDE D7=1), and returning-ace.ts:109 declares turnRate IS PLDELX. Our line 121 `Math.sign(turnRate) === ace.side` is therefore literally the ROM's DEATH predicate used as the SURVIVAL predicate. Inverted. Our own header (:27) flags this polarity as INFERRED; the source settles it against us.
corrections: NOT a one-character fix as claimed. Flipping `===` to `!==` also invalidates the RED contract that pins the current polarity: tests/core/returning-ace.test.ts:60 states the polarity as prose, :268 asserts `check(ace(1), 40, 0.99)` (same-sign) evades, and the AC-6 block at :282-320 is built on same-sign. tests/core/lives.test.ts:397-399 also drives beginPass/evadeCheck. Tests + the two header comments (returning-ace.ts:17-27) move with the code.

A REAL SUBTLETY THE FINDING MISSES: ENSIDE is the sign of the plane's DISPLAY X (PLSTAT+9) captured at the moment of the fly-by (P.UPD0, :2733-2735) — NOT the side the plane entered from. The plane WEAVES across screen centre between spawn and the pass, so those two sides can differ. returning-ace.test.ts:234-240 wires `beginPass(e.side)` straight from enemy.spawn()'s entry side, which is not ENSIDE. Fixing the polarity while still feeding the entry side keeps a second, independent bug alive.

reasoning: Read EOLSEQ in order (RBARON.MAC:1081-1094) and the control flow is exactly as the finding states, in exactly this order: (1) :1081-1083 wrong side → 20$ (death). (2) :1084-1087 `LDA PLDELX / JSR SPABS / CMP I,1C / BCC 20$ ;PLAYER DIES` — soft turn (|PLDELX| < 0x1C = 28) → death OUTRIGHT, no coin flip. (3) ONLY then :1088-1089 `BIT BEFLAG / BPL 17$ ;FIRST TIME FREE` (BEFLAG D7 clear → 17$ = survive, and 17$ SETS the flag to -1). (4) :1090-1091 `JSR RANDOM / BMI 20$ ;AFTER - ONLY 50/50`. So the skill test is the GATE and the freebie/50-50 sits BEHIND it. Our returning-ace.ts:118-125 is the exact mirror: firstPass short-circuits to 'evaded' before any skill test (evadeCheck(ace, 0, 0.99) evades — a player standing still), and a failed skill test falls through to a free 50/50 the ROM never grants. Verified, not taken on faith.
corrections: THE FINDING UNDERSTATES IT — BEFLAG IS GAME-SCOPED, NOT PER-ACE. BEFLAG is written in exactly one place in the whole shipped source (RBARON.MAC:1093, `STA BEFLAG` = -1) and is never explicitly cleared. It is cleared only by the game-start block clear at RBARON.MAC:5959-5963 (`LDX I,EAWRTE-OBJKLD / STA ZX,OBJKLD ;CLR GAME START VARIABLES`), and BEFLAG (:128) sits inside OBJKLD..EAWRTE. It is NOT in GMINIT/INITIAL's clear (:1237-1241 clears only PLDELX..MESTM2, and PLDELX is at :164 — after BEFLAG). So the arcade grants ONE freebie PER GAME, persisting across lives and across every returning ace. Our beginPass() re-arms `firstPass: true` on EVERY pass, so every ace gets its own freebie. The restructure must lift BEFLAG out of ReturningAce into game-scoped state — that is a bigger change than 're-nest evadeCheck's three branches'.

SPELL OUT THE TRUTH TABLE so the implementer cannot keep our 'guaranteed shake': wrong side OR |PLDELX| < 0x1C → HIT (100 %, always). Correct side AND hard turn → first time in the game: EVADE; every time after: 50/50. A perfect break-turn is NEVER a guaranteed evade after the freebie is spent — returning-ace.ts:123's 'bank hard to the correct side — guaranteed shake' is wrong even once the branch order is fixed.

reasoning: Checked all four sub-claims. RBARON.MAC:2482 verbatim `PLPOSZ:	.BYTE -4,-10,-20,-30,-40,-50,-60,-70,-80`, hex region (74..6217), no trailing dots, not a macro (plain .BYTE, no operand transform) → -4,-16,-32,-48,-64,-80,-96,-112,-128. NINE entries. Index: PLNZD:2407-2411 `LDA AX,PLNLVL / STA GMLEVL / TAX / LDA AX,PLPOSZ / STA PLSTAT+1B`; GMLEVL is written NOWHERE else in the file (grep: only :2408), and PLNLVL (:2478) tops out at 5 — so exactly six entries (-4..-80) are reachable, as claimed. NEGATIVE and it lowers the depth: :2704-2707 `CLC / LDA PLSTAT+19 / ADC PLSTAT+1B / STA PLSTAT+19 / BCS 52$ / DEC PLSTAT+1A` is a 16-bit add of a sign-extended negative byte. Ramp 4 → 80 = 20x. Ours (returning-ace.ts:54) is [8,10,13,16,20]: five entries, 2.5x. And the dead-code claim holds under my own grep: NO module under src/ imports returning-ace.ts (only tests/core/returning-ace.test.ts and tests/core/lives.test.ts:66 do), so closeSpeed()/PLPOSZ are unreachable from the game; enemy.ts:60-161 uses its own invented CLOSE_SPEED = 8, flat at every level. The ROM's level ramp genuinely does not exist in our game. All four sub-claims survive.
corrections: THE 'SIGN' SUB-CLAIM IS NOT A DEFECT — drop it. The ROM ADDS a negative delta to the depth; our closeSpeed() returns a positive MAGNITUDE and enemy.ts:161 SUBTRACTS it (`Math.max(enemy.depth - CLOSE_SPEED, MIN_DEPTH)`). Same direction of travel, opposite sign convention. Only the MAGNITUDES (4,16,32,48,64,80 vs 8,10,13,16,20) and the LENGTH (6 reachable vs 5) are wrong. 'Wrong in every respect' is 3-for-4, not 4-for-4.

PLPOSZ IS THE DISPLAY-DEPTH DELTA, NOT THE CLOSING RATE. It is stored to PLSTAT+1B and consumed ONLY at :2676 and :2704-2707, both against the DISPLAY depth (PLSTAT+19/+1A). The MOTION depth (PLSTAT+4/+5) — the one the P.MNDP fly-by trigger tests (:2722-2726) — advances by the separate PLNZD Z-DELTA (PLSTAT+10/+11, :2412-2442), which is roughly an order of magnitude larger. The reasoning's 'the arcade gets 20x harder to line up a shot on' is about apparent size/closing VISUALS, not about how fast the plane reaches you. Our port has one depth; whoever ships this must decide which ROM rate that single depth represents, and say so — wiring PLPOSZ to a corrected 4224-deep world gives a ~94 s approach at level 0.

The magnitudes are only in-unit AFTER EN-002 lands. 4/frame is 4 units out of a 4224-deep world; our 8/frame is 8 out of 1080. 'Level 0 is 2x too fast' is true only in corrected ROM units — in PROPORTIONAL terms our level-0 approach is ~7.8x too fast today. Ship EN-014 with EN-002 or the numbers are meaningless.

The table our port needs is SIX entries (GMLEVL 0..5), not five. Note returning-ace.ts:52's stated rationale ('.LEVLS = 5 entries') is a category error: .LEVLS=5 (RBARON.MAC:504) governs the WINDOW tables (P.OLIM/P.ILIM, where P.WINDW:2756-2759 explicitly clamps GMLEVL 5 → index 4). PLPOSZ is indexed by raw GMLEVL with NO clamp.
